# Feature: MVE Dialog Duration Chips (Header WPM + Footer Audio)

<!-- auto-generated by /implement on 2026-07-12 — derived from .qa/design/mve-dialog-duration-chips.md -->

## Intent

Dialog clips show **estimated speech duration** (WPM, live while typing) as a chip in the header row, and **actual bound-audio duration** as a separate chip in the waveform footer. Both use `HH:MM:SS` via `formatDurationHms`. Header and footer durations are clearly separated (estimate vs. real clip length).

## Preconditions

- Local `.scriptony` project open with MVE / Hörspiel timeline
- At least one dialog text block in Structure & Beats audio lane
- Desktop runtime (`npm run dev:desktop`)

## Happy Path

- [ ] Non-empty dialog text → header shows WPM duration chip (`HH:MM:SS`) next to „Audio: Dialog“
- [ ] Header chip uses pill styling consistent with `MveEmotionChip` (`MveDurationChip`)
- [ ] Header chip `title` / accessible label indicates „Geschätzt (WPM)“
- [ ] Duration updates live while typing in the textarea
- [ ] Bound audio clip (generate/upload/record) → footer shows right-aligned audio duration chip (`HH:MM:SS`) over waveform or loading placeholder
- [ ] Footer chip reflects `clip.endSec - clip.startSec` (updates after trim)
- [ ] No bound audio → footer still shows „Kein Audio“ (unchanged)

## Edge Cases

- [ ] Empty or whitespace-only textarea → no header duration chip
- [ ] Compact clip tier (<120px width) → header chip hidden; footer audio chip still visible when clip bound
- [ ] Text with `--tag` tokens → tags excluded from WPM word count (strip before `estimateDurationSec`)
- [ ] Bound audio + edited text → header shows WPM estimate, footer shows real audio length (both may differ)

## Regression

- [ ] Waveform SVG and loading placeholder bars unchanged when peaks missing
- [ ] Emotion chip, toolbar, textarea editing, and lane drag behavior unchanged
- [ ] Text-only blocks (no audio clip) → no footer duration chip; header chip behaves as above

## Assumptions

- WPM uses project `readingSpeedWpm` (same as clip width estimate)
- `HH:MM:SS` always includes hours segment (e.g. `00:01:30`)
- No new npm dependencies; chip surface reuses `MVE_EMOTION_CHIP_SURFACE_CLASSES`

## Implementation Notes

- New: `MveDurationChip.tsx`, `stripMveTagsFromTextForDuration` in `tags.ts`
- Prop chain: `AudioTimelineMveDialogSegment` → `MveDialogClipHost` → `MveDialogClipCard` → `MveDialogClipWaveformFooter` (`audioDurationSec`)
- Tests: `MveDurationChip.test.tsx`, footer audio chip, optional tag-strip unit test
